sentences.text: any of these… any of these metrics, data points, calculations are coming from, let us know, because we can trace it all back to the source.
So, all this information is coming from publicly available data, and all of this information has to do with the fact that we're tracking unit-level data every single day.
I was just saying, is that what you're doing?
What units come on and come off that must have an app, they come back on?
I mean, is that….
Exactly right, yeah, so if a unit's available on the website today, it's under the available unit bucket.

If tomorrow it's gone from the property website, we're going to assume that somebody put in an application on it, and then if that unit does not reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled, then it becomes a lease.
And here's what I would say, too.
As you're kind of looking at this information and reviewing your own data, it's easy to kind of track the life cycle of a unit.
So if you want to click into this, like, you can drill into all this.
Like, we're saying at, at your property right now, you've got 12 units available.
You can click into here and see those 12 specific units.
You know, we're also saying that you had 3 leases over the last 7 days.
We can show you those 3 specific units.
So really easy to kind of track and follow each unit.
And the only caveat with this that I would say is that, internally, you guys might process an application in 3 days or 4 days.
So you might move it over to a lease quicker than we do.
We have to have that 7-day grace period.
Just because we're not integrated with your property management software.
We don't necessarily, you know, know.
That's private data.
So, like I said, any questions at all, reach out.
I mean, we can… we can trace everything.

… Pricing and availability here, I mean, pretty self-explanatory, but what I would say is, if you want, you can look at this based off of the cheapest one-bedroom or cheapest two-bedroom in the comp set, you can look at it based off of average or absolute minimum.
You can also group the data by metric if you prefer, so… You know, personal preference here.
Fees and deposits are included in here.
As well as amenities, broken out by in-unit and then community level.
So, that's the market survey there.
Lisa, at the top here, this is gonna be, like, your main navigation.

So think of market survey, high level, then we're gonna go to floor plan level data, unit level data, and then historical, so kind of like a cascading effect, but floor plan level data, I think the major call-outs here is that, one, we're going to present floor plan level data two different ways.
So, one, actually showing you each unique floor plan in your comp set.
And two, just giving you the roll-ups.
So, depending on how granular you want to get at the floor plan level, just know both options are available.
Same as before, you can drill into all this data, so you can see the four studios, at least, at Falls Village over the last 7 days if you want.
You can pick and choose your time frame in regards to rent trends as well.
So, again, depending on the conversation you're having, I mean, make sure you're looking at a relevant time frame.
And then, also at the top here, just being able to toggle between gross and net effective rents.
Could you refresh my memory?
Are you guys using a pricing tool, or is that all done manually?
Internally, on an internal tool.
Okay.
So, Bill… Is there anything that they can print that looks like something we use that Mark and Jim would like at quarterlies, or is it all just online?
No, the export, I'm still… the… they… at the quarterlies, they still like to… I can't… What did you say?

I mean, we have ownership that wants… they like it in the format that we've been doing it for years and years and years, and, you know, it needs to be kind of… I mean, obviously, I've logged on, but they're not going to log into this and… and go and drill down.
They just want to see what's the rent, what's the special, where do we stand, and, you know, so is there a report that's going to show that?
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, and, like, your current format, is it PDF or is it Excel?
I guess, what's the current format look like?
So….
Yeah, so let me show you what our pre-canned reports look like, and again, you can download these whenever you want, you can automate them to be delivered to an inbox.
And also, if you want to show them the data as of today, or if you want to show, like, a quarterly aggregate, you can pick and choose that.
Okay.
But the way this works is you'll get a cover page, so this would pretend 1200 Broadway's their property, and then down below here, there's gonna be 6 different data tabs.
One being that market survey that I just walked you through.
So it exports through, looking pretty nice, clean, professional, but all the data points will be included there.
What else I like is we have this visual market survey.
So this can be looked at the property level, we're broken down by floor plan, but it's essentially a dozen or so visuals breaking out every major KPI that they'd be interested in.
So very easy to see where they stack up versus the competition.

We also have two floor plan reports, so one being that roll-up, just showing you, you know, the average studio or the average one-bedroom price, you know, their leasing activity over whatever time frame you choose.
You can also look at that breakdown, like I was saying.
If they've got 10 different one-bedrooms in the property, maybe their smaller one-bedrooms are outperforming their larger one-bedrooms.
So that extra level of granularity is included.
And then what else I like is if you have an, you know, a pretty analytical group, we actually provide you with all of the raw unit-level data behind what's powering these reports.
So if they want to do further analysis, manipulation, create pivot tables, we make it very easy for them to access.
So, lots of data in here, … I will let you know, if you guys have a… that specific report or format that you want, we can get you, like… we call it, like, Data Sync.
I mean, we can basically get you all this raw data, and you could format it to pull from there, if you'd like, so….
Do something new, we should maybe… let's at least let him try it new.
Mine's always my philosophy, though, so…
PDFs, just so you can see them, I think they're a little bit less dynamic, but….
… You brand it with your company's logo, and then you get, like, a comp summary here, just like a map breaking out where each property's located.
Some details on the property, which includes Google reviews, management company, you know, how strong our system thinks a comp is.
And then you get just high-level market summary information.
How strong it is compared to a file.
Based off of our algorithm, so based off of 5 factors, which include, distance from your property.
Okay.
Vintage, total units, pricing, and amenities.
Yeah, so it's… I don't know, I say it's a pretty objective measurement, but at the end of the day, it's not an end-all, be-all.
It's, you know.
You know your markets.
So, like I said, this gives you, like, floor plan level analysis.
You get the average of all the studios in the market, you get the details for those properties that have studios available in them.
And then we do have a nice concession breakdown here as well.
Sorry, there's quite a few floor plans here.
Just some concession details, so you can see which properties are offering concessions, the details behind them.
Okay.
So, yeah, but I mean, if you guys… I'll say this, I mean, if there's any specific formatting or, like.
you know, extra data points you'd like, let us know.
We're happy to take it to our team.
like, who char- like, utility… is there a utility section?
Like, who charges for water and who doesn't?
Yeah, yep.
Under fees and deposits.
Okay.
Yeah, like this example, internet, pests, trash.
Electric.

So, if those things are… are not included, does it give you an effective rent, or is it just… it just tells you… so if the rent's $1,000, but they have to pay their own water and their own trash, does it come over and give you, like, an effective rent, or that's just on a separate page?
Yeah, so… so our rent, so our gross rents would be… just rent.
They wouldn't include, yeah, like the water or the utilities or anything like that.
Okay.
Yeah, and the net effective would be less a, you know, a rent special.
So, if they're offering… obviously, if they're giving, like, free TVs, or an iPad, or gift cards, that doesn't impact the rent price.
We'll let you know, but we won't include it with the, … The net effective numbers.

In regards to the historicals, so what's nice is we do give you all 5 years' worth of historical data for our database, so you can pick and choose whatever time frame's relevant to you, you can pick your interval, and then the data can be looked at at the property level or broken up by floor plan.
But inside of here, you'll get rent trends, so both gross and net rent.

The way we're visualizing the data is right now we can compare, you know, each individual's property's performance, but if we'd like, we can toggle over and just look at our property's performance compared to the meeting of the comp set.
So, I always… I keep saying it seems like, you know, post-pandemic here, that we're back to some traditional seasonality, so it's been really helpful for our customers.
We even include concession history in here, which, again, can be looked at as a value or percentage of rent.
You can look at supply, Leasing velocity trends.
And also your occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.
What else I'd like, too, is below each one of these visuals, you'll see these expandable, collapsible data tables.

So, it just presents the data in a different way, but, here's a data table, and again, you can drill into all this information, so if you want to see the 15 leases at 209 in July, you can drill in and see those 15 units that they did lease.
So… That's the market survey portion of the tool.
Again, we got your two properties lined up here for the trial.
Very cool.
Yeah.
You also have access to Explore, so this is going to be all market, sub-market data.
But again, you can type in any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county, you can draw your own boundary.
If you'd like, you can type in property names, property addresses, or points of interest, and get all this data based off of a certain radius.
So, inside of here is rent comp data, which includes a 30-day snapshot of performance, averages broken up by unit type, and then also those same historicals that we just touched on.
So, you get rent comp data, you get construction, new supply pipeline information, so you can see what's under development in the area.
What's nice is looking at the map there, you can put your mouse over the pins and get some details on the construction project.
Over here on the left, you can see how many units and properties make up each stage of construction, as well as when they're slated to be delivered, plus some details on the sizes of the project.
Finally is demographics, such as population, education, income, and employment data.
And what I've… I think I might have said this before, but what I understand is that unique about our demographic data is that you can actually take it down to, like, a neighborhood or zip code rail.
So if you want to understand a specific, you know, part of town, it obviously can tell a much different story than just looking at the entire market or sub-market as a whole.
So….
Pretty, pretty slick map.
I mean, you can bounce around, you can click into different areas.
Data populates based off of wherever you click.
But, again, that navigation's on the left here, so you got My Properties and Explore.
An amazing amount of data.
Amazing.
Yeah, I'd say play around in here.
Like I said, you can click or drill into just about everything, but again, any questions at all, ask Jamie, ask myself, we're happy to get back to you right away.
But I think one of the cool things about this is we've built this tool so it's really intuitive.
It should be super easy to use, whether it be a site team that's not necessarily in the weeds every day, up to, you know, even analysts or… you know, people that are living in Excel.
Great question, specific to, Falstaff Manors, huh?
So, one of our competitors is just not showing up.
I… searched, by name, it's not showing up.
It's not showing up on the map.
And, What is it?
Ivy Mount Apartments.
And so, it's asking me to get, I guess, find the property's URL in order to add that property, but is there a reason why it just doesn't… A property would just not show up?
Is it location?
It's… It's just not even… Yeah, nothing is showing up when I… when I put it in the search bar.
Give me a second.
Did you call me?
I assume Ivy Mount is two words.
I got it in mind.
So, the issue is, in our system, IV mount… we have IV mount as one word.
I just added it to your concept.
Okay.
So, it was just a matter of the spelling.
Yeah.
I would say, yeah, if you ever can't find a property, like, there's sometimes, like, say you call it, like, the Arbor Apartments, you know, we might have, like, as Arbor Apartments in there.
address, and it'll come up with the address.
Yeah, or whatever fact that is, yeah.
Yeah.
It's nice, I like it.
So, Camille, if you refresh your screen, that should be in there now.
Okay.
Thank you.
But if you come across that again, and you have issues, just email me, and I'll get it in there for you.
Okay, let's… and when does this… the trial run out?
So we initially had talked about you guys using this for a few weeks, I guess just from a usage standpoint, to test it out to see if it's a good fit for you all, how much time do you think you need?
Well, I don't have any of mine on so far, so I'm behind the eight ball, but I'm, you know, willing to go along with whatever

when do you have… because I think the… my opinion, the biggest test is, at a quarterly, you talk about… I mean, we've never shown them anything, and, like, that could have been really helpful for me today in marketing for the point at Arrowhead, but… I mean, so it's just… I mean, ownership hasn't seen any of the reports.
We haven't replaced any of our comparables that we look at at owner's meetings with your reports to even… because they're going to want the ones that decide if we're going to use it or not.

So, I feel like we probably need… I mean, I haven't even been on the dashboard, haven't seen the reports, nothing, so I… I can't speak to whether I like it or not, but we're not going to be the ultimate decision makers.
So, until we put it in front-facing with them.
It's… it's not… it… it's not gonna be… they're not gonna go, yes, let's spend all of this.
And then I guess my other question would be, so does it have to be all-inclusive for the entire portfolio, or can we do it regionally?
Or individually, or is the price based on assuming that we're adding our whole portfolio?
Good question.
So, to answer your first question, no, you do not have to bring on your entire portfolio.
The, the rate that I had initially quoted you of the 8,500 units, 50 cents per unit per month, that was based on the whole portfolio, but you do not need to bring on your entire portfolio.
Okay.
If you feel that there's some other properties you would like to add to your trial, I'm more than happy to do that.
I know in one of the emails, … in the one email that.
You would have caught it back.
But you had asked about Jack.
Oh my god.
I'd like to add the point at Arrowhead, which is in Jacksonville.
I mean, I don't speak for you, but I feel like you probably want to see that.
You know, and quite frankly, I mean, I love the dashboard and everything, but We are such active regionals that, like, we're out in the field.

I'm worried right now about covering and hiring for, like, 3 different properties right now before I leave for vacation on Thursday, so although the reports look great, like, I know for me, I'm not going to be able to sit down and really dive in, nor do I have, you know, not to sound negative, but I don't have time to sit down right now and go through any of that dashboard until maybe the week after next.
And so… I mean, and that's just being, like I said, not negative, but just realistic in what I'm facing that's more urgent for me right now.
So…
When's the next set of quarterlies?
I mean, it would be great… I didn't see any, maybe it would be nice to have a report there, and you could look at it.
September is… Yeah, so I have Jacksonville in September, the end of September, though.
September, yeah.
That would be a time, maybe, to… it'll be top of mind, you guys will have them.
You normally go over them then, right, anyway, so… Yeah, but I think until we show them towards the end.
Really… What's… Bye.
Why wouldn't we, in marketing, bring it up and show them?
And then….
We could do that there, too.
Like I said, that would have been helpful, because I was looking everyone up for the point at Arrowhead this morning.
So yeah, we could do that, too.
Whatever your two weeks one is.
Maybe you bring it up then.
Yeah, so it would be April 9th.
Okay, April.
September 9th.
Okay.
That's a long way away.
No, I'm just kidding.
And then… we could use it for our quarterlies.
I mean, I have one on the 10th, and then, well, 2, and then 3 on the 12th.
But none of the ones that you're sampling right now.
No.
Right.
Nope.
So, Don, if you have a couple other properties that you could find this beneficial for those meetings, I'm more than happy to add those in for you.
Just send me over the property names, and I can get those listed in.
Okay.
I will add in the Point at Arrowhead and Jacksonville for you.
If there are specific comps, if you could email those over, otherwise our system will just generate comps, and then you could either add and remove.
That was a little bit tricky, so I'd be interested to see what your system comes up with.
Of course.
Because it's really, like, and that's a struggle right now with….

some of our marketing initiatives, because it's in a neighborhood called Arrowhead, but we really like to, like, our competition is more in Mandarin, but we don't show up in the searches under Mandarin, because it's not really our zip code.
So that one is really tricky from a marketing standpoint, so it'll be interesting to see what your data… what it comes up with.
Yep, absolutely.
And for all these upcoming meetings, right now, we can do a tentative, just a 30-day trial to give you all access.
You can download the reports you need, access the dashboard, play around with it.
At least that gives you time to come back from your vacation to access without having to rush or anything like that, so why don'.
At the end of September, I think it would be perfect.
I'll leave it on for 30 days.
So, why don't I do this then?

Why don't we add all of Jacksonville, because we'll have owner… ownership meetings there on the 22nd and 23rd, the 23rd and 24th of September, so that might give us a whole… and that area's… struggling right now as a whole, the Jacksonville area, … So… maybe doing a sampling of all of those communities, then I can present them at all of those… at those meetings, if that's not too big of an ask.
How many properties is that?
Bye.
Okay.
If you could just send those over to me, that would be great.
Okay.
And I can do 4, I have one that's not struggling as bad, so I'll send you 3 others.
Okay.
That's great.
What I'll plan to do, I understand that you'll be on vacation, but I will plan to reach out to you all in the next couple of weeks just to touch base, see how things are going.
However, if questions come up in the meantime, between now and then, please do not hesitate to reach out to us.
If you have issues adding comms or just questions about the data, please reach out.
I will make a note to reach back out in 2 weeks, and then from there, we can touch base to see how it's going, and then we'll plan on leaving the trial live for 30 days.
Okay, great.
Perfect.
And Lisa, do you need my email address?
… You should be good now, if you want to email her.
Try it real quick, Jamie, if you can, right?
I did that last time, Bill, and I emailed her, and it bounced again.
Yes.
Is it because I mind-casted it?
Yes.
Well, I didn't know.
No, that's fine, I'm just… that's why, yeah.
Did you try again?
I'll make sure, I'll go into Mimecast right now to look.
Hold on just a minute.
an email.
Just Lisa on a one-off.
I have… I have something from you.
I have an invite from you.
Perfect.
That was… That wasn't quite from her, that was from… Oh, never mind.
Try… yeah, maybe you should try.
I am emailing you, it's just called Test Email.
I just sent it.
I gotta jump to another call, but it was good seeing everybody.
Hi, Jocelyn, thank you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Oh, wait, …
Test email, a waiting index, you should have it.
Perfect.
Okay, great.
Okay, so if you want to respond to that, Lisa, with the 4 other… or three other properties, plus the point at Arrowhead, we'll get those into the system.
Don, you said you had one or two others that you wanted to add in?
Yes.
You could just email those to me as well, that would be nice.
Okay.
Our team will add them in.
If you have specific comps, feel free to give me those as well, just so you don't have to waste your time adding them yourself.
Our team will do all of that for you.
Okay.
Great.
Thank you, gentlemen.
Any other questions?
I'm still sit down.
Zip.
We're all set?
Yep, I think so.
Alright, I'll call to email those, we'll get those added in, and then, Bill, I will plan on reaching out to the three of you in two weeks, just to check in.
That sounds good.
Thanks, Shane.
Thanks!
